title: Coryphopteris athyriocarpa
intro: Coryphopteris athyriocarpa, also known as a type of fern, is a species of plant in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms associated with fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Coryphopteris athyriocarpa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coryphopteris athyriocarpa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including bronchitis and fever, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
taxonomy: Coryphopteris athyriocarpa is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Coryphopteris, which is part of the division Pteridophyta. The classification of Coryphopteris athyriocarpa can be broken down further into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Tracheophyta. This fern is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. Further classification into family, genus, and species level is a more specific description of its taxonomic hierarchy.
